headline: Dryden Gold Drills 32.87 g/t Au Over 4.25m — THREE NEW Zones at Gold Rock Including 252 g/t Bonanza
snippet: Dryden Gold (TSX-V: DRY) hit three new high-grade zones at Gold Rock, Ontario: 4.25m @ 32.87 g/t Au incl. 0.50m @ 252 g/t, plus 3.80m @ 13.08 g/t and 4.00m @ 6.51 g/t — all 300m step-outs northeast from known zones.

headline: SILVER CRASHES to $73.58 — US-Iran Peace Talks Collapse, Trump Signals Hormuz Blockade
snippet: Silver (XAG/USD) tumbled 3.5% to $73.58 on April 13 after US-Iran talks in Islamabad collapsed and Trump signalled military measures targeting the Strait of Hormuz, spiking the USD and stoking oil-driven inflation fears.

headline: Allied Gold Shareholders GREEN-LIGHT Zijin's C$5.5B Takeover — China Locks In African Gold Portfolio
snippet: Allied Gold (TSX: AAUC) shareholders cleared Zijin Gold International's C$5.5B all-cash bid at C$44/share (27% premium), adding ~400koz/year including the 'generational' Kurmuk project in Ethiopia targeting production in H2 2026.

headline: F3 Uranium Drills 13m @ 0.28% U3O8 at Tetra Zone — Broach Discovery Doubles Strike Length
snippet: F3 Uranium (TSX-V: FUU) extended the Tetra Zone at Broach in Saskatchewan's Athabasca Basin, returning 13.0m @ 0.28% U3O8 with a 3.0m sub-interval @ 1.19% U3O8 — more than doubling the mineralized strike length to 270m.

headline: Denison's Phoenix ISR Gets CNSC Construction Licence — First Large Uranium Mine Approved in Canada in 20+ Years
snippet: The CNSC issued Denison Mines (TSX: DML) a construction licence for Wheeler River's Phoenix ISR project in Saskatchewan — Canada's first ISR uranium mine and the first large-scale uranium mine approved in the country in over two decades.

headline: LITHIUM DOUBLED in Q1 2026 to $26,278/Tonne — Battery Storage Demand Up 55%, Surplus Narrowing Fast
snippet: Battery-grade lithium carbonate surged to $26,278/tonne in Q1 2026 as energy storage demand jumped 55% year-on-year. Analysts now project a supply deficit of up to 80,000 tonnes LCE as planned projects fail to keep pace with grid-storage growth.

headline: McEwen Copper Eyes $4B Financing for Los Azules — US EXIM Bank and Export-Credit Agencies in Talks
snippet: McEwen Copper Inc. is in discussions with global lenders and export-credit agencies including the US EXIM Bank to structure $4B in financing for the Los Azules copper project in Mendoza, Argentina — one of the world's largest undeveloped copper deposits.

headline: Wesdome Wins Quebec Approval for Kiena's Presqu'ile Zone — Production AHEAD of 2026 Schedule
snippet: Wesdome Gold Mines (TSX: WDO) secured an updated Certificate of Authorization and mine lease for the high-grade Kiena Presqu'ile Zone in Val-d'Or, Québec — with stope production commencing ahead of the original 2026 schedule.

headline: China's Rare Earth Magnet Controls THREATEN U.S. Defense Supply Chains — 7 Elements Now Restricted
snippet: CSIS warns China's controls on seven heavy rare earths — including terbium and dysprosium — and their derivative magnets threaten US defense procurement. A temporary Nov 2026 suspension does not affect April 2025 restrictions already in force.

headline: Gold-to-Silver Ratio Flashes BULLISH at 105+ — History Says Sharp Silver Outperformance Follows
snippet: The gold-to-silver ratio peaked above 105 in April — a historically extreme reading. GoldSeek SWOT analysis notes every prior contraction from this level has coincided with significant silver outperformance, even against adverse macro backdrops.

headline: Mining M&A MEGA-DEALS Rose 68% in 2025 — $139B in Transactions Sets Stage for 2026 Super-Cycle Wave
snippet: Mining Beacon tallied 91 mining and metals M&A deals totaling $139B in 2025 — with billion-dollar mega-deals rising 68% from 2024. Gold drove activity, with high valuations enabling share-based acquisitions; analysts expect consolidation to accelerate in 2026.
